content.text: The wall. Two branches — Guardian (protect allies: intercept, shield, zone control) and Juggernaut (punish attackers: thorns, counter-attacks, escalation). Abilities complement the augment system, providing active tools for threat generation, positioning, and emergency protection.

Hold the Line (Passive): Enemies that start their turn adjacent to you cannot move away without provoking an opportunity attack from you (even if they have abilities that normally prevent opportunity attacks). Additionally, your opportunity attacks stop the target's movement — they don't just take damage, they stay where they are. You are a wall. Nothing gets past without your permission.
T2 — Trained Techniques (300 cr each, 1 downtime)

rows: Target one enemy within Close range. That enemy MUST attack you on their next turn if they are able to (can reach you, have attacks available). If they cannot reach you, they must move toward you. Lasts 1 round. The core threat generation tool.

Melee attack (Chrome vs Evasion). 1d6 damage + push target 1 tile in any direction. If the target collides with a wall or another creature, both take 1d4 bonus damage. Positional control. Requires a shield.
If you don't move on your turn, gain +2 Armor until your next turn. Standing ground is rewarded. Stacks with Subdermal Plating and worn armor.

Once per round, when you take damage from a melee attack, you deal your Chrome bonus as damage back to the attacker (armor-ignoring). Passive, automatic. At Chrome 10 (+3 bonus), every melee hit costs the attacker 3 HP.
Enemies within Close range have -1 accuracy against all targets. Your proximity is unsettling. Passive aura — just standing near enemies makes them worse.
Once per encounter: when you take damage from a single attack, reduce it by your Grit bonus + 2 (after armor). Free action, declared after seeing the damage. Emergency mitigation.

content.text: T3 — Advanced Techniques (600 cr each, 2 downtime)

rows: All enemies within Close range MUST target you on their next turn if able (mass Taunt). All allies within Close range gain +1 accuracy on their next attack. Once per encounter. The “everyone look at me” button.

Choose one ally within Close range. Until your next turn, all attacks against that ally are automatically redirected to you (no Reaction cost, unlimited uses). You shadow them — everything aimed at them hits you instead.
Until your next turn, no enemy can move through any tile adjacent to you. Period. Not with extra AP, not with special abilities, not with teleportation. Absolute zone denial for 1 round.

For 2 rounds: all damage you take is reduced by 3 (flat, after armor, stacks with everything). At the end of 2 rounds, take 1d6 unavoidable damage as the suppressed kinetic energy releases. Borrow time.
When you drop below 25% HP: for 2 rounds, all your melee attacks deal +1d6 bonus damage and you gain +2 accuracy. Automatically triggers, free action. The wounded animal is the most dangerous.
Heal 1d6 + Grit bonus HP. Once per encounter. Your only self-heal — and it costs half your turn.

Move up to your full movement AND make a melee attack at the end of the movement. If you moved at least 10ft in a straight line, the attack deals +1d4 bonus damage and pushes the target 1 tile. The Fortress's engage tool — close distance and hit in one action.

content.text: T4 — Master Techniques (1,000 cr each, 3 downtime)

rows: For the rest of the encounter: your Intercept no longer costs a Reaction (unlimited uses per round). All allies within Close range gain +2 Armor. You become the squad's permanent shield. Once per encounter.

You cannot be stunned, feared, charmed, or have your position forcibly changed by any effect. If an effect would stun you, you instead take 1d4 damage (your body absorbs the shock). Total condition immunity for movement/control conditions that cripple tanks.

Your Counter-Strike (T3 augment) and Retaliatory Strike (T2 ability) no longer have a once-per-round limit. Every melee hit against you triggers a response. Enemies that swarm you in melee are attacking a blender.
When you would gain a Scar from dropping to 0 HP, roll a Grit check (DC 8). On success: no Scar. You still drop to 0 HP and need revival, but your body holds together. The long-campaign tank passive.

While you are alive and conscious, all allies can see enemy HP ranges and active conditions (your HUD shares targeting data). Additionally, allies within Close range cannot be critically hit. Your presence stabilizes the entire squad.
